meta_title: For Freelancers: analytics setup to Solve can't finish projects (Beginner Case Study)
meta_description: Learn how to analytics setup and overcome can't finish projects. A Beginner-level Case Study using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Freelancers: Analytics Setup to Solve Project Stall
content.seo_description: Stop leaving projects unfinished. Use this beginner-friendly guide to master analytics setup with Antigravity and Claude Code to deliver client value fast.
content.answer_block: The inability to finish projects often stems from a lack of measurable progress. By implementing a lightweight, automated analytics layer, you transform vague project goals into data-backed milestones. This setup uses Antigravity to scaffold the infrastructure and Claude Code to integrate tracking, ensuring you ship, measure, and iterate quickly.
content.intro:
The graveyard of unfinished freelance projects is paved with good intentions and missing feedback loops. You start with high energy, but without a pulse on how your code performs or how users interact with your features, the momentum dies. You aren't failing because you lack skill; you're failing because you're flying blind.

True professional velocity comes from visibility. When you integrate analytics early, you stop guessing and start building based on reality. This case study strips away the complexity, showing you how to wire up professional-grade tracking in minutes, turning your 'in-progress' status into a 'shipped' success.

steps.title: Define Tracking Schema
steps.description: Before writing code, define the core user actions that signal project completion. This focuses your development effort.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a tracking schema for a SaaS project that includes user signup, feature activation, and churn events. Output as a clean JSON structure.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'create a tracking_schema.json file based on the provided requirements'

steps.title: Initialize Antigravity Analytics Layer
steps.description: Use Antigravity to scaffold the boilerplate code for your analytics provider, avoiding manual SDK configuration.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Scaffold an analytics wrapper for PostHog that handles event batching and user identification patterns.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'apply the generated analytics wrapper to the src/lib directory'

steps.title: Inject Tracking Hooks
steps.description: Automate the insertion of tracking hooks into your primary UI components to capture user intent.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a React hook 'useAnalytics' that wraps tracking events with error handling and console logging for development.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'find all primary CTA buttons and wrap their onClick handlers with useAnalytics'

steps.title: Implement Performance Monitoring
steps.description: Capture load times and runtime errors to ensure the project remains stable and professional.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Add a performance monitoring middleware that logs page load duration and JavaScript heap usage.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'integrate the performance monitoring middleware into the main app entry point'

steps.title: Automated Deployment Verification
steps.description: Use Claude Code to verify that events are firing correctly in a staging environment before the final handoff.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a test script that simulates user flow and verifies event payloads reach the analytics dashboard.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'execute the event verification test script and report success or failure'

pro_tips: Always track the 'Session Duration' metric; it is the single best indicator of whether a feature is actually solving a user's problem.
Use feature flags in your analytics to toggle tracking on/off without needing a full redeploy.
Ignore vanity metrics like 'page views'; focus exclusively on 'Conversion' and 'Retention' events to prove value to clients.
Automate your analytics reporting by piping event data into a Slack channel for real-time project health updates.
Keep your tracking payload lean; excessive metadata will degrade your application performance and frustrate users.
content.next_steps: Once your analytics are live, set a 7-day review period. Use the data collected to identify one feature that users ignore, remove it, and ship the refined version to your client.
content.conversion_block: Stop letting your projects stall in the 'almost done' phase. By building with data-first architecture, you increase your billable value and your project completion rate. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
